evidence: FermatPsp.lean:63 `def FermatPsp (n b : ℕ) : Prop` — composite n passing Fermat's test to base b, coprime to b; exact defn of Wikidata's Fermat pseudoprime.
FermatPsp.lean:55 `def ProbablePrime (n b : ℕ) : Prop` is the base predicate FermatPsp specializes; docstring explicitly links it to Fermat pseudoprimality.
SmoothNumbers.lean:240 `def smoothNumbers (n : ℕ) : Set ℕ := {m | m ≠ 0 ∧ ∀ p ∈ primeFactorsList m, p < n}` — textbook smooth-number definition, no prior QID tag exists.
GaussSum.lean:72 `def gaussSum (χ : MulChar R R') (ψ : AddChar R R') : R' := ∑ a, χ a * ψ a` matches Wikidata's Gauss sum (mult. char × add. char over finite ring), not currently mapped.
JacobiSum/Basic.lean:47 `def jacobiSum (χ ψ : MulChar R R') : R' := ∑ x : R, χ x * ψ (1 - x)` is exactly the classical Jacobi sum of two multiplicative characters; module cites Ireland-Rosen Ch.8.3.
EllipticDivisibilitySequence.lean:91 `def IsEllDivSequence : Prop := IsEllSequence W ∧ IsDivSequence W` — module docstring explicitly names 'elliptic divisibility sequences (EDS)'.
EllipticDivisibilitySequence.lean:289 `def normEDS (n : ℤ) : R` is the canonical normalized EDS built from IsEllDivSequence; standard object of the same Wikidata concept.
AbelSummation.lean:129, docstring literally "Abel's summation formula."; general statement of the sum-to-integral identity, matches Q26001323 exactly.

Ostrowski.lean:465 `theorem equiv_real_or_padic ... f.IsEquiv real ∨ ∃ p, f.IsEquiv (padic p)` is precisely Ostrowski's classification of absolute values on ℚ; module header names it 'Ostrowski's Theorem'.

MahlerMeasure.lean:110, docstring "**Northcott's Theorem:** the set of integer polynomials of degree ≤ n and Mahler measure ≤ B is finite" — direct statement about Mahler measure, the Wikidata concept.
Niven.lean:123 `theorem niven ... cos θ ∈ {-1,-1/2,0,1/2,1}` — module header '# Niven's Theorem'; exact statement matches Q4116459 (rational angles with rational cosine).
VonMangoldt.lean:65 `noncomputable def vonMangoldt : ArithmeticFunction ℝ` — module docstring '# The von Mangoldt Function', notation Λ matches Wikidata description exactly; currently untagged.

Liouville.lean:27 `def liouville : ArithmeticFunction ℤ` — module docstring '# The Liouville Function', λ(n)=±1 by parity of prime factors; decl already carries Q140744/Q205243/Q205966 but not Q385019 (the function itself).

Carmichael.lean:48 `def carmichael : ArithmeticFunction ℕ` — docstring '# The Carmichael function', exponent of unit group of ZMod n; decl has Q140744/Q389208 but not the Carmichael-function QID itself.
PrimeCounting.lean:57 `def primeCounting (n : ℕ) : ℕ` — module docstring '# The Prime Counting Function', standard π(x); decl carries other QIDs but not Q251922 for the function concept itself.
LegendreSymbol/Basic.lean:109 `def legendreSym (a : ℤ) : ℤ := quadraticChar (ZMod p) a`; decl already carries Q878259 (quadratic residue) but not Q748339, the Legendre-symbol object itself.
AlmostPrime.lean:40 `def IsAlmostPrime (k n : ℕ) : Prop` — n has exactly k prime factors w/ multiplicity; module docstring links to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Almost_prime, matching Q936614.
AlmostPrime.lean:49 `abbrev IsSemiprime (n : ℕ) : Prop := IsAlmostPrime 2 n` — exactly Wikidata's semiprime (product of two primes, counted with multiplicity).
FactorisationProperties.lean:60 `def Abundant (n : ℕ) : Prop := n < ∑ i ∈ properDivisors n, i` — exact textbook definition, verified against Wikidata description word-for-word.
FactorisationProperties.lean:64 `def Deficient (n : ℕ) : Prop := ∑ i ∈ properDivisors n, i < n` — exact match to Wikidata's deficient number.

FactorisationProperties.lean:74 `def Weird (n : ℕ) : Prop := Abundant n ∧ ¬ Pseudoperfect n` — matches Wikidata desc 'abundant number that is not semiperfect' exactly (pseudoperfect=semiperfect here).
FactorisationProperties.lean:69 `def Pseudoperfect (n : ℕ) : Prop` — n equals sum of a subset of its proper divisors; exact match to Wikidata's pseudoperfect number.
